snapshot.goal: Transition EHR from passive data repository to active clinical partner through unified AI system integrating ambient documentation, decision support, and automated administration
snapshot.current_state: Functional voice-interactive demonstration system with React frontend and Claude API backend showcasing diabetes management workflow for synthetic patient
snapshot.target_users: Healthcare providers in ambulatory primary care and internal medicine settings
snapshot.key_metrics: 94.2% adoption rate in reference implementations, $540,000 annual value per physician, 64% documentation time reduction
snapshot.timeline: Not specified in sources, reference implementations require 6-12 months for production deployment with 90-day gated phases
snapshot.evidence: [Source: purpose file lines 1-6; MANUSCRIPT_FINAL_54_FIGURES.md Chapter 13 implementation outcomes; ai_ehr_demo_v2.tsx implementation; MANUSCRIPT_FINAL_54_FIGURES.md Chapter 2 time savings]

workflows.trigger: Voice command 'What's on my schedule today?' or automatic on day start
workflows.inputs: Today's patient schedule, critical lab results, pending decisions
workflows.outputs: 60-120 second summary identifying 3-4 high-priority clinical issues
workflows.pain_points: Physicians unprepared for complex patients, surprises during encounters, inefficient prioritization
workflows.evidence: [Source: Complete_Deployment_Guide Test 1; ai_ehr_demo_v2.tsx handleMorningBriefing; WORKFLOW_VERIFICATION_CHECKLIST.md Step 1]

workflows.trigger: Voice command 'Prep me on [patient name]' before entering exam room
workflows.inputs: Complete patient medical history, recent labs and imaging, medication changes, prior visit notes
workflows.outputs: 60-90 second patient-specific briefing with clinical trajectory, current medications, critical issues, and decisions needed
workflows.pain_points: Chart pre-review time consuming, information scattered across EHR, missing critical context during encounters
workflows.evidence: [Source: WORKFLOW_VERIFICATION_CHECKLIST.md Step 2; Complete_Deployment_Guide Test 2; ai_ehr_demo_v2.tsx handlePreVisitBriefing]

workflows.trigger: Voice commands during patient visit for medications, orders, clinical questions
workflows.inputs: Natural language physician commands, patient context, clinical knowledge base
workflows.outputs: Medication orders with dosing, evidence citations, drug interactions, safety guidance, lab orders with clinical rationale
workflows.pain_points: Documentation interrupts patient interaction, medication details not memorized, evidence retrieval disruptive, safety checking manual
workflows.evidence: [Source: WORKFLOW_VERIFICATION_CHECKLIST.md Step 3; clinical_knowledge_base.js decision support functions; Complete_Deployment_Guide Tests 3-5]

workflows.trigger: Voice command 'Document this visit' after encounter completion
workflows.inputs: Conversation history with all clinical decisions, patient data, problem list, medication changes, orders placed
workflows.outputs: Complete SOAP note with HPI, physical exam, labs reviewed, assessment and plan for each problem, medication changes, follow-up plan, billing justification
workflows.pain_points: Documentation consumes 5.9 hours daily, after-hours charting common, copy-paste errors, incomplete notes, billing code selection difficult
workflows.evidence: [Source: WORKFLOW_VERIFICATION_CHECKLIST.md Step 5; ai_ehr_demo_v2.tsx handleDocumentation; MANUSCRIPT_FINAL_54_FIGURES.md documentation burden evidence]

workflows.trigger: Sequential voice commands switching between patients during clinic session
workflows.inputs: Multiple patient contexts, separate conversation histories per patient
workflows.outputs: Patient-specific responses maintaining separate state, accurate recall when switching back to prior patient
workflows.pain_points: Cognitive load managing multiple complex patients, context-switching errors, losing track of decisions made

in_scope: Ambient AI documentation with voice interaction
Clinical decision support with evidence citations
out_of_scope: Live patient data and PHI processing
ambiguous: Extension to inpatient or ED workflows
scope.evidence: [Source: purpose file Core Principles section lines 7-15 and Security notice lines 45-47; AI Model Development table showing deferred features with placeholder logic; workflow scope implied from WORKFLOW_VERIFICATION_CHECKLIST and Complete_Deployment_Guide test scenarios]

stakeholders.decision_rights: Final authority for all clinical decisions, workflow design input, AI recommendation acceptance or rejection
stakeholders.responsibilities: Use system during patient care, provide feedback, validate medical accuracy, review and sign documentation
stakeholders.evidence: [Source: MANUSCRIPT_FINAL_54_FIGURES.md human-AI partnership principles and physician decision authority]

stakeholders.decision_rights: Resource allocation approval, competing priority resolution, strategic direction
stakeholders.responsibilities: Visible leadership support, budget authorization, barrier removal, organizational commitment
stakeholders.evidence: [Source: MANUSCRIPT_FINAL_54_FIGURES.md Phase 1 stakeholder engagement and leadership commitment assessment]

stakeholders.decision_rights: Workflow design authority, peer influence, pilot selection
stakeholders.responsibilities: Peer advocacy, troubleshooting support, co-design participation, early adoption
stakeholders.evidence: [Source: MANUSCRIPT_FINAL_54_FIGURES.md champion identification and development, Phase 1 readiness]

stakeholders.decision_rights: Day-to-day execution decisions, issue escalation, support coordination
stakeholders.responsibilities: Orchestrate eight stakeholder groups using Conductor Model, manage timeline, coordinate training and support
stakeholders.evidence: [Source: MANUSCRIPT_FINAL_54_FIGURES.md Conductor Model for distributed leadership]

stakeholders.decision_rights: Technical integration approach, EHR workflow optimization
stakeholders.responsibilities: Evaluate AI products, ensure EHR integration quality, validate clinical data models
stakeholders.evidence: [Source: MANUSCRIPT_FINAL_54_FIGURES.md informaticist role in technical teams]

stakeholders.decision_rights: Technology selection within constraints, implementation approach
stakeholders.responsibilities: Software engineering, data science, security implementation, infrastructure management

stakeholders.decision_rights: Consent for encounter recording, data use preferences
stakeholders.responsibilities: Not explicitly defined for demonstration but production requires consent and feedback mechanisms
stakeholders.evidence: [Source: MANUSCRIPT_FINAL_54_FIGURES.md patient privacy concerns and consent policies]

requirements.requirement: Convert physician-patient conversation to structured SOAP note with HPI, physical exam, labs, assessment, and plan
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Complete note generated in under 30 seconds, includes all medication changes from conversation, proper medical documentation format, billing justification included
requirements.dependencies: Speech recognition, patient data access, Claude API availability
requirements.evidence: [Source: purpose file Zero-Template Documentation principle; WORKFLOW_VERIFICATION_CHECKLIST.md Step 5; ai_ehr_demo_v2.tsx handleDocumentation]

requirements.requirement: Provide evidence-based medication suggestions for diabetes with CKD considering patient factors
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Response under 60 seconds, cites specific trials like FLOW 2024, identifies contraindications, provides dosing guidance
requirements.dependencies: Clinical knowledge base, patient medication list, renal function data
requirements.evidence: [Source: clinical_knowledge_base.js decision support functions; WORKFLOW_VERIFICATION_CHECKLIST.md Step 4 evidence-based support]

requirements.requirement: Identify contraindications and interactions between proposed medications and current drugs
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Detects critical interactions, provides severity classification, recommends monitoring or alternatives
requirements.dependencies: Current medication list, drug interaction database
requirements.evidence: [Source: clinical_knowledge_base.js medication_safety function; WORKFLOW_VERIFICATION_CHECKLIST.md drug interaction test]

requirements.requirement: Maintain accurate current medication list updated in real-time during conversations
requirements.acceptance_criteria: AI lists all medications including those just added showing 'Ozempic just added' and 'lisinopril 40mg just increased' proving state management
requirements.evidence: [Source: WORKFLOW_VERIFICATION_CHECKLIST.md Step 4 and Critical Memory Test requiring state persistence]

requirements.requirement: Accept natural language lab order commands and confirm placement
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Voice command 'Order A1C and BMP in 6 weeks' confirmed with clinical context like fasting requirements
requirements.dependencies: EHR integration for order entry, laboratory system connectivity
requirements.evidence: [Source: Complete_Deployment_Guide Test 6 and Voice Command Quick Reference]

requirements.requirement: Synthesize longitudinal patient data into concise pre-visit briefing
requirements.acceptance_criteria: 60-90 second briefing includes trajectory, medications, labs, and decisions needed
requirements.dependencies: Complete patient history access, recent results

requirements.acceptance_criteria: Not fully specified, described as simple rule-based function
requirements.dependencies: Quality measure specifications, completed screening data
requirements.evidence: [Source: purpose file Care Gap Prediction showing placeholder logic line 58]

nfrs.requirement: Morning briefing under 120 seconds, patient prep 60-90 seconds, medication orders under 45 seconds, clinical questions under 60 seconds, documentation under 30 seconds
nfrs.threshold: Specific timing requirements are acceptance criteria
nfrs.evidence: [Source: WORKFLOW_VERIFICATION_CHECKLIST.md timing requirements for each workflow step]

nfrs.requirement: 99.9-99.99% uptime for clinical systems with ambient AI requiring higher 99.99%
Sub-second response times for real-time clinical workflows
nfrs.threshold: Millisecond response for time-critical decision support

nfrs.requirement: Human-in-the-loop for all clinical decisions, AI provides recommendations not autonomous actions
EXPLICITLY EXCLUDED from demonstration - would require defense-in-depth security, encryption, RBAC, HIPAA compliance for production
nfrs.threshold: Not applicable to demonstration, production requires full HIPAA technical safeguards
nfrs.evidence: [Source: purpose file lines 45-47 excluding security; MANUSCRIPT_FINAL_54_FIGURES.md Security Architecture for production requirements]

nfrs.requirement: Comprehensive logs of all PHI access and AI recommendations for production
nfrs.threshold: Sufficient detail to reconstruct access patterns during investigations
nfrs.evidence: [Source: MANUSCRIPT_FINAL_54_FIGURES.md Audit Logging and Breach Notification requirements]

nfrs.requirement: 7-year medical record retention typical, audio files create storage challenges with 3.75 petabytes annually for 500-physician organization
nfrs.threshold: Not specified for demonstration, production requires regulatory compliance

nfrs.requirement: Performance monitoring detecting degradation, automated alerts for latency thresholds, capacity planning
nfrs.threshold: Not specified, production requires continuous monitoring

nfrs.requirement: Realistic first-year investment $50,000-100,000 per 100 physicians for production
nfrs.threshold: Includes licensing $200-400 per physician monthly plus implementation and support costs

architecture.description: Demonstration uses simplified React client with direct Claude API calls, production requires dual-repository architecture separating operational and analytical systems

modules.responsibilities: User interaction via voice and text, conversation display, mode switching between workflows
modules.inputs: User voice commands or text, patient selection
modules.evidence: [Source: ai_ehr_demo_v2.tsx UI components; MANUSCRIPT_FINAL_54_FIGURES.md Experience Layer]

modules.responsibilities: Request routing, service coordination, API authentication - SIMPLIFIED in demo to direct API calls
modules.outputs: Coordinated responses from knowledge and model layers
modules.interfaces: Simplified - eliminated in demo, production needs API gateway
modules.evidence: [Source: purpose file component simplification table; MANUSCRIPT_FINAL_54_FIGURES.md Orchestration]

modules.responsibilities: Clinical knowledge base with medication info, trial evidence, dosing guidelines, drug interactions
modules.interfaces: JSON structure accessed by AI via prompt context

modules.responsibilities: Patient information storage - in-memory for demo, dual-repository for production
modules.inputs: Patient data updates from clinical actions
modules.outputs: Complete patient context for AI queries
modules.interfaces: In-memory JSON in demo, would require FHIR APIs for production

modules.responsibilities: AI inference using Claude Sonnet 4 for clinical reasoning and text generation
modules.inputs: System prompts with patient context and clinical knowledge

modules.responsibilities: EHR connectivity, terminology mapping - NOT IMPLEMENTED in demo
modules.inputs: Would receive data from EHR via FHIR APIs
modules.outputs: Would write orders and documentation back to EHR
modules.evidence: [Source: MANUSCRIPT_FINAL_54_FIGURES.md Multi-Vendor Integration Architecture; purpose file noting simplified demo excludes this]

architecture.evidence: [Source: purpose file architecture section; ai_ehr_demo_v2.tsx implementation; MANUSCRIPT_FINAL_54_FIGURES.md comprehensive architecture chapters]

interoperability.status: Decided - using FHIR-like JSON internally for demo, full FHIR required for production
interoperability.details: Demo simplifies strict validation, production requires multi-vendor FHIR profiles
interoperability.evidence: [Source: purpose file lines 35-36; MANUSCRIPT_FINAL_54_FIGURES.md FHIR implementation]

interoperability.status: Not used - legacy standards being replaced by FHIR
Required for production, synthetic data uses standard codes
interoperability.details: Mapping challenges when EHRs use institutional codes

interoperability.status: Undecided which vendor to target first
interoperability.details: Epic uses App Orchard, Cerner uses open APIs, others need custom development
interoperability.evidence: [Source: MANUSCRIPT_FINAL_54_FIGURES.md EHR Integration at Enterprise Scale]

interoperability.status: Decided - Claude API (Anthropic) for current implementation, purpose file stated Gemini
interoperability.evidence: [Source: purpose file line 41 vs ai_ehr_demo_v2.tsx implementation]

data_flows.description: Data flows from user interaction through AI processing to response presentation with state updates

entities.attributes: Visit reason, chief complaint, date/time, clinical decisions needed
SOAP structure with subjective, objective, assessment, plan
Name, dose, frequency, route, dates, adherence status
Test name, value, reference range, date, trend

flow_sequence: User interaction via voice or text input
System retrieves patient context from data layer
AI model receives complete patient data and clinical knowledge in prompt
Model generates response with reasoning and evidence
Response flows to experience layer for presentation
Clinical actions update patient data with state management
For production: orders and notes flow through integration to EHR
data_flows.phi_locations: All patient data in demonstration is synthetic and explicitly excludes PHI, production would have PHI in patient demographics, clinical notes, labs, medications
data_flows.phi_protection: Excluded from demo, production requires encryption at rest and in transit, RBAC, audit logging
data_flows.evidence: [Source: ai_ehr_demo_v2.tsx queryAI function; purpose file workflow description; MANUSCRIPT_FINAL_54_FIGURES.md security sections]

ai_capabilities.inputs: Audio or text transcript of encounter, patient context
ai_capabilities.outputs: Structured SOAP note with all elements
ai_capabilities.guardrails: Physician review required, confidence scoring, error tracking
ai_capabilities.human_decision: Physician must review and sign all documentation
ai_capabilities.evidence: [Source: MANUSCRIPT_FINAL_54_FIGURES.md Ambient AI technical workflow; WORKFLOW_VERIFICATION_CHECKLIST.md documentation requirements]

ai_capabilities.inputs: Complete medical history, recent labs, medication changes
ai_capabilities.outputs: 60-90 second patient briefings with trajectory and decisions needed
ai_capabilities.guardrails: Time limits, citation requirements, focus on actionable information
ai_capabilities.human_decision: Physician determines which recommendations to act upon

ai_capabilities.inputs: Patient problems, current meds, labs, allergies, insurance
ai_capabilities.outputs: Ranked treatment options with dosing, evidence, contraindications, monitoring
ai_capabilities.guardrails: Display contraindications, require physician confirmation, drug interaction checks
ai_capabilities.human_decision: Physician must confirm before any prescription executed
ai_capabilities.evidence: [Source: clinical_knowledge_base.js decision support; WORKFLOW_VERIFICATION_CHECKLIST.md medication ordering]

ai_capabilities.outputs: Severity classification, drug pairs, clinical issues, actions
ai_capabilities.guardrails: Clinical decision support at prescription entry, alert fatigue management
ai_capabilities.human_decision: Physician can override with documented reasoning

ai_capabilities.inputs: Problem list, completed screenings, quality measures
ai_capabilities.guardrails: Physician review before patient contact, appropriateness consideration
ai_capabilities.human_decision: Physician determines if measure appropriate for individual patient
ai_capabilities.evidence: [Source: purpose file Care Gap Prediction as placeholder logic]

safety_validation.existing_approach: Enterprise validation through pilot testing measuring accuracy, confidence scoring, error tracking, user acceptance testing
safety_validation.proposed_plan: Use de-identified synthetic patients, test medication dosing in renal impairment, drug interaction detection, allergy checking, GLP-1 recommendations, hallucination controls comparing AI output to source data, bias checks across demographics, error taxonomy with remediation, human-in-the-loop review, rollback strategy with manual backup
safety_validation.test_datasets: Synthetic patients from encounter_generator matching diabetes, CKD, polypharmacy scenarios
safety_validation.safety_cases: Metformin contraindications eGFR <30, drug interactions ACE-I with K-sparing diuretics, GLP-1 contraindications, clinical reasoning validation
safety_validation.documentation: Audit logs, provenance tracking AI-generated vs human, version control, rationale for overrides
safety_validation.evidence: [Source: MANUSCRIPT_FINAL_54_FIGURES.md validation approaches; clinical_knowledge_base.js safety functions; proposed plan synthesized from safety principles in sources]

risks.indicators: Error rates in testing, physician override frequency, near-miss reports
risks.mitigation: Mandatory physician review, drug interaction checking, renal dosing, comprehensive testing, immediate investigation of safety events
risks.owner: TBD - CMO or Clinical Safety Officer
risks.evidence: [Source: clinical_knowledge_base.js safety functions; MANUSCRIPT_FINAL_54_FIGURES.md quality infrastructure]

risks.likelihood: N/A for demo excluding PHI, high for production without proper controls
risks.indicators: Unauthorized access attempts, security assessment findings
risks.mitigation: Defense-in-depth security, encryption, RBAC, audit logging, vendor agreements
risks.evidence: [Source: purpose file lines 45-47; MANUSCRIPT_FINAL_54_FIGURES.md Security Architecture]

risks.impact: High when clinicians can't access AI during patient care
risks.indicators: Integration test failures, API response degradation, data sync errors
risks.mitigation: Phased testing, abstraction layers, automated testing, vendor management, manual fallbacks
risks.evidence: [Source: MANUSCRIPT_FINAL_54_FIGURES.md noting integration as most common delay cause]

risks.likelihood: Moderate based on documented healthcare patterns
risks.indicators: Override rates increasing, response delays, satisfaction declining
risks.mitigation: Intelligent filtering, severity stratification, alert tuning, effectiveness monitoring

risks.risk: Workflow disruption from AI adding burden
risks.indicators: Negative feedback, declining adoption, increased task time, workarounds
risks.mitigation: Co-design workshops, pilot testing with iteration, one-click actions, context-aware AI, feedback loops

risks.impact: Moderate-to-high for financial and regulatory exposure
risks.indicators: Denied claims, audit findings, code discrepancies
risks.mitigation: Physician verification, regular audits, documented rationale, coding specialist involvement
risks.owner: TBD - Compliance Officer or RCM Director
risks.evidence: [Source: assumption based on standard compliance practices]

risks.indicators: Declining accuracy, increasing edit rates, inappropriate recommendations
risks.mitigation: Continuous monitoring, regular retraining, A/B testing, version control
risks.owner: TBD - Data Science or AI Engineering lead

risks.indicators: Unfavorable renewal terms, pricing increases, limited interoperability
risks.mitigation: Abstraction layers, contract provisions for data portability, architectural flexibility

risks.indicators: Missing audit trails, incomplete provenance, internal findings
risks.mitigation: Comprehensive audit logging, provenance tracking, retention policies, regular audit testing

decisions.decision: Use simplified Flask/React architecture for demonstration
decisions.rationale: Focus on AI functionality rather than infrastructure complexity

decisions.decision: Exclude HIPAA compliance and PHI from demonstration
decisions.rationale: Simulation environment using synthetic data allows rapid development
decisions.documentation: Purpose file security section with prominent notice

decisions.decision: Use Claude API via Anthropic (vs Gemini stated in plan)
decisions.rationale: Not explicitly stated, potentially superior clinical reasoning
decisions.documentation: Inconsistency between purpose file and implementation

decisions.decision: Focus on diabetes patient with medication non-adherence
decisions.rationale: Demonstrate realistic clinical complexity in manageable scope
decisions.documentation: Patient database structure and workflow checklist

decisions.decision: Implement state management for multi-turn dialogue
decisions.rationale: Essential for realistic clinical workflow requiring AI memory

decisions.decision: Use React for frontend (vs HTML in plan)
decisions.rationale: Not explicitly stated, likely richer state management and UX
decisions.documentation: Implementation in ai_ehr_demo_v2.tsx vs purpose file
decisions.evidence: [Source: purpose file line 73 vs actual implementation]

decisions.decision: Include comprehensive clinical knowledge base with trials
decisions.rationale: Demonstrate decision support sophistication beyond simple lookup
decisions.evidence: [Source: clinical_knowledge_base.js with trial data and decision functions]

build_plan.mvp_definition: Functional voice-interactive clinical demonstration system deployable as Custom GPT or React web application showcasing core AI-EHR integration for single-patient ambulatory diabetes scenario
build_plan.mvp_done_criteria: Successful completion of 8-test verification protocol: morning briefing under 120s, patient prep 60-90s, medication order under 45s with evidence, state persistence across commands, SOAP note under 30s, multi-patient management, voice quality

epics.deliverables: Validated synthetic patient database with Sarah Mitchell complete record
epics.tasks: Define JSON schema, populate Sarah Mitchell data, generate additional patients if needed, validate completeness
epics.owner: TBD - clinical informaticist or physician champion
epics.checkpoints: Data validation review by clinical stakeholder
epics.completion_evidence: JSON database with clinical reviewer sign-off
epics.risk_link: Patient safety risk requiring accurate test data

epics.deliverables: Comprehensive clinical knowledge with diabetes management, GLP-1s, evidence base, drug interactions
epics.tasks: Catalog required knowledge, research trials, implement decision support functions, encode interactions, add renal dosing, validate accuracy
epics.owner: TBD - clinical informaticist with endocrinology expertise
epics.checkpoints: Clinical accuracy review by diabetes physician
epics.completion_evidence: Knowledge base JSON with SME approval

epics.deliverables: Functional AI query engine with Claude API, prompt engineering, error handling
epics.tasks: Select provider (done), implement API key management, design prompt template, implement state management, add error handling, test quality, tune prompt
epics.owner: TBD - AI engineer or full-stack developer
epics.completion_evidence: API integration passing WORKFLOW_VERIFICATION quality criteria

epics.deliverables: Complete workflow functions for briefing, prep, queries, orders, documentation
epics.tasks: Implement morning briefing, pre-visit prep, room entry summary, query handling, medication changes, lab orders, documentation generation

epics.deliverables: Conversation state tracking persisting decisions across dialogue
epics.tasks: Implement conversation history, update patient data on changes, pass history to AI, implement multi-patient isolation, test memory
epics.completion_evidence: Memory test passing without state confusion
epics.risk_link: Patient safety ensuring accurate medication lists

epics.deliverables: Polished React interface with schedule, modes, voice/text input, conversation display
epics.tasks: Implement ScheduleView, create mode switching, build conversation display, add voice input, implement text input, add action buttons, apply styling
epics.owner: TBD - frontend developer or UX designer

epics.deliverables: Comprehensive test execution and documentation covering all protocols and safety cases
epics.tasks: Prepare test data, execute 8-test protocol, document results with timing, run safety tests, test edge cases, hallucination checks, clinical review, document failures
epics.completion_evidence: Completed checklist, results spreadsheet, clinical sign-off, issue list

epics.deliverables: Deployed system with complete documentation and demonstration scripts
epics.tasks: Deploy to hosting or create Custom GPT, test deployed system, write deployment docs, create demo scripts, develop troubleshooting guide, prepare user guide
epics.completion_evidence: Deployed system, documentation package, successful demonstrations, feedback collected

build_plan.timeline: Week 1-2 Epics 1-2 parallel, Week 3 Epic 3, Week 4-5 Epics 4-5, Week 6 Epic 6, Week 7 Epic 7, Week 8 Epic 8. Critical path: Epic 1→3→4→7→8
